meta.title: X590 Battery Replacement Cost UK — Compare Quotes
meta.description: Jaguar I‑Pace X590 battery and motor replacement costs — 90 kWh battery, twin motors, 400 PS. Real UK data, reliability insights, and battery health guidance from 15+ vetted EV specialists.

mainEntity.name: How much does a Jaguar I‑Pace X590 battery replacement cost in the UK?

acceptedAnswer.text: A replacement 90 kWh traction battery for the I‑Pace typically costs between £9,000 and £35,000 supply only, depending on whether you go through a Jaguar main dealer or an independent EV specialist. Used battery packs are available from £5,000–£10,000. Fitting adds £800–£1,500 in labour. Use our calculator for a personalised figure based on your car's age and condition.

mainEntity.name: Which Jaguar I‑Pace X590 powertrain component is the most reliable?

acceptedAnswer.text: The permanent‑magnet synchronous motors (front and rear) are inherently robust and have few reported mechanical failures. Most motor‑related issues are sensor or wiring harness faults — not the motor itself. The 90 kWh LG Chem battery is the component that requires the most attention, particularly on pre‑2021 cars. Post‑2021 cars have improved thermal management software.

mainEntity.name: What are the common problems with the Jaguar I‑Pace X590?

acceptedAnswer.text: The most serious issue is HV battery thermal events on pre‑2021 model‑year vehicles — documented cases of smoke or fire from the battery area. Motor position sensor or wiring harness failure can cause loss of drive and transmission errors. The 12V auxiliary battery also drains prematurely on many examples. Software updates have addressed many early‑car issues.

mainEntity.name: Is the Jaguar I‑Pace X590 reliable?

acceptedAnswer.text: The motors themselves are reliable — permanent‑magnet synchronous designs are proven technology. The battery, however, is the X590's biggest question mark. Early (2019–2020) cars have documented thermal management concerns that have led to safety recalls. Post‑2021 cars are significantly improved. A full battery health check is essential before purchase, and extended warranty cover is strongly recommended.

mainEntity.name: Which Jaguar I‑Pace X590 should I avoid?

acceptedAnswer.text: We don't say 'avoid' outright — but we consistently advise against pre‑2021 I‑Paces without a documented battery health check and proof that all safety recalls (H441, H459, H471) have been performed. A 2019 car with no battery service history is a gamble we wouldn't take. Post‑2021 cars are a much safer bet.

mainEntity.name: How do I know if my I‑Pace X590 powertrain needs replacing?

acceptedAnswer.text: Transmission errors, loss of drive, 'gearbox error' warnings, shaking during acceleration, or inability to engage drive or reverse are signs of motor or sensor issues. Battery warnings, reduced range, or thermal warnings indicate battery problems. A 12V battery that keeps dying despite replacement suggests a deeper electrical issue.

mainEntity.name: Is it worth replacing a Jaguar I‑Pace X590 battery, or should I scrap the car?

acceptedAnswer.text: Depends on the car's value and whether it's under warranty. Against a well‑kept £30,000 post‑2021 I‑Pace under warranty, a battery replacement is covered — no decision required. Against a tired £15,000 pre‑2021 example, a £9,000‑£20,000 battery replacement crosses our 60% Jaguar threshold — we'd advise walking away and buying a newer car instead.

mainEntity.name: How long do Jaguar I‑Pace X590 powertrains last?

acceptedAnswer.text: The motors are capable of 200,000+ miles with proper maintenance — they're proven technology with few wear parts. The battery's longevity depends on usage patterns, charging habits, and thermal management. Expect 8–10 years (100,000–150,000 miles) before significant degradation on a well‑maintained post‑2021 car. Pre‑2021 cars have less predictable longevity.

mainEntity.name: What is the difference between the I‑Pace X590 and other Jaguar models?

acceptedAnswer.text: The X590 is Jaguar's only all‑electric vehicle — it shares no powertrain components with any other Jaguar model. It's built on the D7e platform, manufactured by Magna Steyr in Austria. Unlike the XF, XE, or F‑Pace which use internal combustion engines (Ingenium diesel and petrol), the I‑Pace uses twin electric motors and a 90 kWh battery. This page covers the I‑Pace X590 only.

mainEntity.name: How do I find my Jaguar I‑Pace X590 powertrain specifications?

acceptedAnswer.text: The motor code is TZ‑204‑X‑S‑A for both front and rear motors. The battery is a 90 kWh LG Chem unit with 432 pouch cells. Your VIN contains the vehicle's complete specification — any Jaguar specialist can decode it for you. All UK‑market I‑Pace models use the EV400 powertrain (400 PS, 696 Nm). Tell us your VIN and we'll confirm your exact specification.

hero.h1: Jaguar I‑Pace X590 Powertrain — The Complete UK Guide
hero.subHeadline: The Jaguar I‑Pace X590 is Jaguar’s first all‑electric vehicle — twin permanent‑magnet motors producing 400 PS, a 90 kWh battery, and a 4.8‑second 0‑60 sprint. Real UK costs, honest reliability data, and the battery degradation question every EV buyer asks. Backed by 40+ real UK X590 enquiries in 2025 [EM‑VERIFIED].

engineDatabase.h2: Jaguar I‑Pace X590 Powertrain Specifications — The Complete Database
engineDatabase.subHeadline: Every element of the I‑Pace’s electric powertrain — from the dual permanent‑magnet motors to the 90 kWh battery pack that powers them.

confidenceScore.title: How confident are these ratings?
confidenceScore.text: Motor reliability: 90% confidence — permanent‑magnet synchronous motors are inherently reliable; reported issues are typically software or sensor‑related rather than mechanical. Battery reliability: 70% confidence — the 90 kWh LG Chem pack has known degradation concerns and some thermal events reported in early 2019 model‑year vehicles. All ratings marked [PATTERN DATA] pending dedicated EV powertrain pages.

overview.intro: The Jaguar I‑Pace X590 is Jaguar’s first production battery‑electric vehicle, launched in March 2018 with deliveries commencing later that year. Styled by Ian Callum, it was the first premium European electric SUV to rival the Tesla Model X. Built on Jaguar Land Rover’s D7e platform and manufactured by Magna Steyr in Graz, Austria, the I‑Pace combines a 90 kWh LG Chem battery pack with twin permanent‑magnet motors producing 400 PS and 696 Nm of torque. It offers a WLTP range of up to 480 km (298 miles), 0‑100 km/h in 4.8 seconds, and 100 kW DC fast charging.
overview.keyFacts: Platform: JLR D7e • Predecessor: None (new model line) • Successor: Discontinued (2025) — no direct successor announced • Sibling body styles in this generation: None.
overview.marketIntelligenceLine: The Jaguar I‑Pace X590 generated 40 real UK powertrain enquiries in 2025 [EM‑VERIFIED], concentrated on battery health diagnostics and motor issues as early examples pass the 5‑year ownership mark.

items.quote: “Early 2019 and 2020 model‑year I‑Pace battery packs have documented thermal management concerns, with some vehicles experiencing thermal overload resulting in smoke or fire from the HV battery area. 12V battery drain is also a common complaint.” Who it’s for: buyers who insist on a post‑2021 car with extended battery warranty.

“A replacement 90 kWh traction battery can cost anywhere from £9,000 to £35,000, depending on whether you go through a Jaguar dealer or an independent specialist. This is the single biggest ownership cost risk on the I‑Pace.” Who it’s for: owners who budget for extended warranty cover.

“2021‑model‑year I‑Paces received improved battery management software and thermal control updates, significantly reducing the risk of battery issues. They also benefit from the Pivi Pro infotainment system, addressing one of the early car’s biggest criticisms.” Who it’s for: buyers who want I‑Pace ownership without the early‑adopter battery anxiety.

“The I‑Pace’s motors are often treated as a single unit, but failures can occur on one axle independently. Replacing a single motor (around £2,500–£5,000) is far cheaper than a full powertrain replacement. Most owners don’t realise this is an option.” Who it’s for: buyers facing a motor fault who want the cheapest viable repair.

economicsRule.title: The X590 rule
economicsRule.text: A £9,000–£35,000 battery replacement against a £15,000–£45,000 I‑Pace sits at 20–230% of the car’s value. On a sub‑£20,000 early I‑Pace, a battery failure is almost certainly a write‑off — our 60% Jaguar threshold is crossed immediately. On a post‑2021 car under warranty, the risk is dramatically lower. Buy with warranty, budget for extended cover, and treat battery health as the single most important pre‑purchase check.

problems.title: HV Battery Thermal Events (pre‑2021)
problems.rootCause: A concern with the capability of diagnostic software introduced through safety recalls has been identified — post‑software‑update 2019 model‑year vehicles have experienced thermal overload, showing as smoke or fire from the underside where the HV battery is located.
problems.recommendation: If you’re considering a pre‑2021 I‑Pace, verify that all safety recalls (H441, H459, H471) have been performed and that the battery has passed a full health check. A post‑2021 car is significantly safer. On any pre‑2021 car without a recent battery inspection, walk away.

cta.label: Compare I‑Pace X590 battery health checks →

problems.title: Motor Position Sensor / Wiring Harness Failure
problems.affectedModels: Jaguar I‑Pace X590 all model years.
problems.rootCause: The motor position sensors or wiring harness can fail, leading to transmission errors, loss of drive, or “gearbox error” warnings. Symptoms include shaking, inability to engage drive or reverse, and error codes P0C50‑56 or P0A40‑00.
problems.recommendation: At the first sign of transmission errors or loss of drive on an I‑Pace, get a specialist diagnostic immediately — a £500–£1,500 sensor or harness repair is dramatically cheaper than the £2,500–£5,000 motor replacement that follows if ignored.

cta.label: Compare I‑Pace X590 motor repair quotes →

problems.title: 12V Battery Drain
problems.affectedModels: Jaguar I‑Pace X590 all model years.
problems.rootCause: The 12V auxiliary battery can drain prematurely due to software issues, parasitic drain from infotainment systems, or simply age. A dead 12V battery can prevent the HV battery from engaging, leaving the car immobile.
problems.recommendation: Replace the 12V battery every 3‑4 years as preventative maintenance on any I‑Pace — it’s a £100–£250 job that prevents the far more disruptive “dead car” scenario.

cta.label: Compare I‑Pace X590 12V battery replacement quotes →

replacementCosts.h2: X590 Powertrain Replacement Costs — By Component

coreVariants.scopeNote: All UK‑market I‑Pace X590 models use the identical EV400 powertrain — twin permanent‑magnet motors producing 400 PS and 696 Nm, with a 90 kWh battery. Trims affect equipment and interior specification only; powertrain costs and failure patterns are identical across all variants. The First Edition was a launch‑year special. R‑Dynamic was introduced from 2020.

marketIntelligence.averageReplacementCost: £9,000–£35,000 supply only [PLACEHOLDER — confirm vs cost sheet]
mostCommonFailures: 2. Motor position sensor / wiring harness failure
